Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend certain Acts in relation to criminal and correctional matters (child protection, gender-based violence, delays and other measures)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends various Acts in relation to criminal and correctional matters.
It amends the Criminal Code to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: create a new offence that prohibits engaging in a pattern of coercive or controlling conduct toward an intimate partner;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that, in the following circumstances, murder â known as femicide when committed against a female person â is murder in the first degree:

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: the murder is committed against an intimate partner in the context of a pattern of coercive or controlling conduct,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: the murder is committed in the context of sexual violence,

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: the murder is committed in the context of human trafficking, or

Provision.Label: (iv)
Provision.Text: the murder is motivated by hate;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: provide that, if an offender commits manslaughter in those circumstances, the court must consider whether to impose a sentence of imprisonment for life on the offender and, if that sentence is imposed, an adult offender is ineligible for parole for 10 to 25 years;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: remove from the criminal harassment offence the requirement to prove that the victim subjectively feared for their safety and replace it with a requirement to prove that the harassing conduct could reasonably be expected to cause the victim to believe that someoneâs safety is threatened;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: amend the offence of non-consensual distribution of an intimate image to include, among such images, a visual representation showing an identifiable person depicted as nude, as exposing their sexual organs or as engaged in explicit sexual activity, if the depiction is likely to be mistaken for a visual recording of that person;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: amend certain existing child sexual offences to include prohibiting a person from inviting a child to expose their own sexual organs for a sexual purpose;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: criminalize the distribution of visual representations of bestiality;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: create a new offence relating to the recruitment of a person under 18 years of age to be a party to an offence;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: provide that victims of certain offences, such as offences in the commission of which violence was used, threatened or attempted against an intimate partner, are entitled to testimonial aids;

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: permit courts to order that an offender serve a period of imprisonment below a mandatory minimum term of imprisonment, but only if the mandatory minimum term of imprisonment would amount to cruel and unusual punishment for that particular offender;

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: create a new Part establishing a framework for applying alternative measures and restorative justice processes in appropriate cases;

Provision.Label: (l)
Provision.Text: create a new Part in respect of unreasonable delay that requires a court to consider specific factors in relation to case complexity, directs a court to exclude time periods in respect of specific applications and requires that a stay of proceedings be ordered only if a court is satisfied, taking into account a list of factors, that no other remedy would be appropriate and just;

Provision.Label: (m)
Provision.Text: streamline and strengthen the procedural rules in sexual offence trials that govern when evidence of a complainantâs past sexual activity can be adduced and when certain private records, including therapeutic records, can be produced or adduced; and

Provision.Label: (n)
Provision.Text: allow the possibility of using affidavit evidence for certain cases involving identity theft and identity fraud.
The enactment also makes consequential amendments to other Acts.
The enactment also amends the Youth Criminal Justice Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: ensure that it better reflects the Canadian Victims Bill of Rights with respect to the rights and interests of victims;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: modernize the principle requiring consideration of the needs of young persons, including by requiring particular attention to those of Aboriginal and Black young persons; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: allow youth justice courts to order that a young person enter into a recognizance if there is a reasonable fear that the young person will commit a child sexual offence.
The enactment also amends the Canadian Victims Bill of Rights to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: modify the preamble to affirm the importance of victim-centred and trauma-informed approaches;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide victims with the right to be treated with respect, courtesy, compassion and fairness;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: enable victims to receive information without being required to make a request;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide that victims have the right to receive information about their rights under that Act and the protection measures that are available to them;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: broaden the information that victims have the right to receive about available restorative justice processes; and

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: clarify the right of victims to present a victim impact statement at sentencing and a victim statement for consideration when decisions regarding parole or corrections are made about the offender who harmed them.
The enactment also amends the National Defence Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that victims of certain offences, such as offences in the commission of which violence was used, threatened or attempted against an intimate partner, are entitled to testimonial aids;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: create a new Division in respect of unreasonable delay that requires a court martial to consider specific factors in relation to case complexity, directs a court martial to exclude time periods in respect of specific applications and requires that a stay of proceedings be ordered only if a court martial is satisfied, taking into account a list of factors, that no other remedy would be appropriate and just;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: streamline and strengthen the procedural rules to align with the Criminal Code procedural rules in sexual offence trials that govern when evidence of a complainantâs past sexual activity can be adduced and when certain private records, including therapeutic records, can be produced or adduced;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide victims with the right to be treated with respect, courtesy, compassion and fairness;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: provide that victims have the right to receive information about their rights under the Division of the National Defence Act entitled âDeclaration of Victims Rightsâ and information about the protection measures that are available to them; and

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: enable victims to receive information from authorities in the military justice system without being required to make a request.
The enactment also amends An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: clarify the types of Internet services covered by that Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: require that transmission data be provided with the mandatory notification in cases where the material is manifestly child sexual abuse and exploitation material;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: extend the period of preservation of data related to an offence; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: extend the limitation period for the prosecution of an offence under that Act.

The enactment also amends the Firearms Act to clarify that an individual whose firearms licence or registration certificate has been revoked is required to deliver their firearm to a peace officer, firearms officer or chief firearms officer and to provide that an individual is not eligible to hold a licence under that Act if the chief firearms officer has reasonable grounds to suspect that the individual may have engaged in an act of domestic violence or stalking.

The enactment also amends the Corrections and Conditional Release Act to, among other things, enhance the disclosure of information to victims and other components of the criminal justice system and provide for the submission of victim statements in certain instances.

Finally, the enactment also amends the Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Act to facilitate legal assistance between Canada and supranational bodies with responsibility for criminal investigations or prosecutions.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to amend certain Acts in relation to criminal and correctional matters (child protection, gender-based violence, delays and other measures)

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Victims Act.

Subsection.Text: The Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after section 3:

Section.MarginalNote: Violence in commission of offence, including against intimate partner

Subsection.Text: In a provision listed in subsection (2), a reference to an offence in the commission of which violence is used, threatened or attempted against any person or class of persons includes a reference to any of the following offences:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence that is of a sexual nature or that is committed for a sexual purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 264 (criminal harassment);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 279.01 (trafficking in persons);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 279.011 (trafficking of a person under the age of 18 years).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies with respect to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 109(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 110(1) and (2.1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 486.1(1.1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 486.2(1.1);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 486.3(2);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 718.3(8);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: section 726.21;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 729.2(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 810.03(4).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 3.01(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 264.01 (coercion or control of intimate partner);

Section.Text: Subsection 7(4.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Offence in relation to sexual offences against children
Subsection.Text: Despite anything in this Act or any other Act, every one who, outside Canada, commits an act or omission against a person who is under the age of 18, that, if committed in Canada, would be an offence under section 151, 152, 153 or 155, subsection 160(2) or (3), section 163.1, 170, 171, 171.1, 172.1, 172.2, 173, 271, 272 or 273 or subsection 286.1(2) shall be deemed to commit that act or omission in Canada if the person who commits the act or omission is a Canadian citizen or a permanent resident as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 109(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under subsection 85(1) (using firearm in commission of offence), 85(2) (using imitation firearm in commission of offence), 95(1) (possession of prohibited or restricted firearm with ammunition), 99(1) (weapons trafficking), 100(1) (possession for purpose of weapons trafficking), 102(1) (making automatic firearm), 102.1(1) (possession of computer data), 102.1(2) (distribution of computer data), 103(1) (importing or exporting knowing it is unauthorized) or 104.1(1) (altering cartridge magazine) or section 264 (criminal harassment) or 264.01 (coercion or control of intimate partner),

Section.Text: Subsection 113(4) of the Act is amended by replacing âsubsection 810(3)â with âsection 810 or 810.03â.
Section 150 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: sexual organsâinclude breasts that are or appear to be female breasts and the anal region;â(organes sexuels)

Section.Text: Subsection 150.1(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: It is not a defence to a charge under section 153, 170, 171, 172 or 279.011 or subsection 279.02(2), 279.03(2), 286.1(2), 286.2(2) or 286.3(2) that the accused believed that the complainant was 18 years of age or more at the time the offence is alleged to have been committed unless the accused took all reasonable steps to ascertain the age of the complainant.

Section.Text: The portion of section 151 of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is amended by replacing âÃ des fins dâordre sexuelâ by âdans un but sexuelâ.
The portion of section 152 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Invitation to sexual touching or exposure

Subsection.Text: Every person commits an offence who, for a sexual purpose, invites, counsels or incites a person under the age of 16 years to

Paragraph.Text: touch, directly or indirectly, with a part of the body or with an object, their own body, the body of the person who so invites, counsels or incites or the body of any other person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: expose their own sexual organs.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1)
Paragraph 153(1)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: dans un but sexuel, touche, directement ou indirectement, avec une partie de son corps ou avec un objet, une partie du corps de lâadolescent;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 153(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraph (b) by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for a sexual purpose, invites, counsels or incites a young person to touch, directly or indirectly, with a part of the body or with an object, their own body, the body of the person who so invites, counsels or incites or the body of any other person; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for a sexual purpose, invites, counsels or incites the young person to expose their own sexual organs.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 153.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Sexual exploitation of person with disability

Subsection.Text: Every person commits an offence who is in a position of trust or authority towards a person with a mental or physical disability or who is a person with whom a person with a mental or physical disability is in a relationship of dependency and who, for a sexual purpose, counsels or incites that person to, without that personâs consent,

Paragraph.Text: touch their own body, the body of the person who so counsels or incites or the body of any other person, directly or indirectly, with a part of the body or with an object; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: expose their own sexual organs.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of
Section 160 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Every person commits an offence who knowingly publishes, distributes, transmits, sells, makes available or advertises any visual representation that is or is likely to be mistaken for a photographic, film, video or other visual recording of a person committing bestiality.

No person shall be convicted of an offence under subsection (3.1) if the conduct that forms the subject-matter of the charge served the public good and did not extend beyond what served the public good.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Question of law and fact, motives
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (3.2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is a question of law whether the conduct served the public good and whether there is evidence that the conduct alleged went beyond what served the public good, but it is a question of fact whether the conduct did or did not extend beyond what served the public good; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the motives of the accused are irrelevant.

Subsection.Text: Every person who commits an offence under subsection (3.1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 160(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The court may, in addition to any other sentence that it may impose under any of subsections (1) to (3) and (3.4),
The portion of subsection 161(1) of the Act before paragraph (a.1) is replaced by the following:

When an offender is convicted, or is discharged on the conditions prescribed in a probation order under section 730, of an offence referred to in subsection (1.1) in respect of a person who is under the age of 18 years, the court that sentences the offender or directs that the accused be discharged, as the case may be, in addition to any other punishment that may be imposed for that offence or any other condition prescribed in the order of discharge, shall consider making and may make, subject to the conditions or exemptions that the court directs, an order prohibiting the offender from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: attending a public park or public swimming area where persons under the age of 18 years are present or can reasonably be expected to be present, or a daycare centre, schoolground, playground or community centre;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 161(1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: seeking, obtaining or continuing any employment, whether or not the employment is remunerated, or becoming or being a volunteer in a capacity, that involves being in a position of trust or authority towards persons under the age of 18 years;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: having any contact â including communicating by any means â with a person who is under the age of 18 years, unless the offender does so under the supervision of a person whom the court considers appropriate; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 161(1.1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 151, 152, 153 or 155, subsection 160(2) or (3), section 163.1, 170, 171, 171.1, 172.1 or 172.2, subsection 173(2), section 271, 272, 273 or 279.011, subsection 279.02(2) or 279.03(2), section 280 or 281 or subsection 286.1(2), 286.2(2) or 286.3(2);

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 162(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is in a place in which a person can reasonably be expected to be nude, to expose their sexual organs or to be engaged in explicit sexual activity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person is nude, is exposing their sexual organs, or is engaged in explicit sexual activity, and the observation or recording is done for the purpose of observing or recording a person in such a state or engaged in such an activity; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 162(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 162.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 162.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Everyone who, with the intent to intimidate or to be taken seriously, knowingly threatens to publish, distribute, transmit, sell, make available or advertise an intimate image of a person knowing that the person depicted in the image would not give their consent to that conduct, or being reckless as to whether or not that person would give their consent to that conduct, is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: In this section, intimate image means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a visual recording of a person made by any means including a photographic, film or video recording,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the person is nude, is exposing their sexual organs or is engaged in explicit sexual activity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which, at the time of the recording, there were circumstances that gave rise to a reasonable expectation of privacy, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of which the person depicted retains a reasonable expectation of privacy at the time the offence is committed; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation that is made by any electronic or mechanical means and that shows an identifiable person who is depicted as nude, as exposing their sexual organs or as engaged in explicit sexual activity, if the depiction is likely to be mistaken for a visual recording of that person.

Section.Text: Subsection 162.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: When an offender is convicted, or is discharged on the conditions prescribed in a probation order under section 730, of an offence under subsection 160(3.1) or section 162.1, the court that sentences or discharges the offender, in addition to any other punishment that may be imposed for that offence or any other condition prescribed in the order of discharge, may make, subject to the conditions or exemptions that the court directs, an order prohibiting the offender from using the Internet or other digital network, unless the offender does so in accordance with conditions set by the court.
Subparagraph 163.1(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the dominant characteristic of which is the depiction, for a sexual purpose, of a sexual organ of a person under the age of eighteen years;

Subsection.Text: Section 163.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.2):

Every person who, with the intent to intimidate or to be taken seriously, knowingly threatens to publish, distribute, transmit, sell, make available or advertise any child sexual abuse and exploitation material

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 164(1) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A judge may issue a warrant authorizing seizure of copies of any material â including a recording, publication or written material, or representation â if the judge is satisfied by information on oath that there are reasonable grounds to believe that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the material is illicit material; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: copies of the material are kept in premises within the jurisdiction of the court and, in the case of material alleged to be illicit material described in any of paragraphs (a) to (d) of the definition of that term in subsection (8), are so kept for sale or distribution.

Subsection.Text: Within seven days of the day on which the warrant is issued, the judge shall issue a summons to the occupier of the premises requiring them to appear before the court and show cause why the seized thing should not be forfeited to His Majesty.
The owner and the maker of the seized thing that is alleged to be illicit material may appear and be represented in the proceedings to oppose the making of an order for the forfeiture of the thing.

If the court is satisfied, on a balance of probabilities, that the seized thing is illicit material, it may make an order declaring the thing to be forfeited to His Majesty in right of the province in which the proceedings take place, for disposal as the Attorney General may direct.

If the court is not satisfied that the seized thing is illicit material, it shall order that the thing be restored to the person from whom it was seized without delay after the time for final appeal has expired.
Subsection 164(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an order is made under this section by a judge in a province with respect to one or more copies of any material, no proceedings shall be instituted or continued in that province under subsection 160(3.1) or section 162, 162.1, 163, 163.1, 286.4 or 320.103 with respect to those or other copies of the same material without the consent of the Attorney General.
The definitions advertisement for conversion therapy, advertisement of sexual services, intimate image and voyeuristic recording in subsection 164(8) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection 164(8) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any visual representation that is or is likely to be mistaken for a photographic, film, video or other visual recording of a person committing bestiality, as defined in subsection 160(7),

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a visual recording, as defined in subsection 162(2), that is made as described in subsection 162(1),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an intimate image, as defined in subsection 162.1(2), in relation to which an offence was committed under section 162.1,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a publication that is obscene, within the meaning of subsection 163(8),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: child sexual abuse and exploitation material, as defined in subsection 163.1(1),

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any material â including a photographic, film, video, audio or other recording, made by any means, a visual representation or any written material â that is used to advertise sexual services contrary to section 286.4, or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any material â including a photographic, film, video, audio or other recording, made by any means, a visual representation or any written material â that is used to promote or advertise conversion therapy contrary to section 320.103;â(matÃ©riel illicite)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 164.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Warrant of seizure â material on computer system

Subsection.Text: If a judge is satisfied by information on oath that there are reasonable grounds to believe that material, consisting of illicit material or of computer data that makes illicit material available, is stored on and made available through a computer system that is within the jurisdiction of the court, the judge may order the custodian of the computer system to
Subsection 164.1(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the court is satisfied, on a balance of probabilities, that the material is illicit material or computer data that makes illicit material available, it may order the custodian of the computer system to delete the material.
Subsections 164.1(7) and (8) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the court is not satisfied that the material is illicit material or computer data that makes illicit material available, the court shall order that the electronic copy be returned to the custodian of the computer system and terminate the order made under paragraph (1)(b).
Subsections 164(6) and (7) apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to this section.
Section 164.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):
In this section, computer data and computer system have the same meanings as in subsection 342.1(2) and court, illicit material and judge have the same meanings as in subsection 164(8).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 169:

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who â being in a position of trust, power or authority towards a young person and knowing that the young person is under 18 years of age or being reckless as to that fact â recruits, counsels, encourages or invites the young person to be a party to an offence under this Act or the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act if the young person is afterwards a party to that offence or an offence related to that offence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Position of trust, power or authority
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), a person is considered to be in a position of trust, power or authority towards a young person by reason, among others, of being 18 years of age or more.

It is not a defence to a charge under subsection (1) that the accused believed that the young person was at least 18 years of age unless the accused took reasonable steps to ascertain the age of the young person.
Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: In this section, young person means a person under the age of 18 years.
Subparagraph 171.1(5)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the dominant characteristic of which is the depiction, for a sexual purpose, of a personâs sexual organs;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 171.1(5)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: written material whose dominant characteristic is the description, for a sexual purpose, of explicit sexual activity involving a person; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an audio recording whose dominant characteristic is the description, presentation or representation, for a sexual purpose, of explicit sexual activity involving a person.

Subsection.Text: Section 171.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
For greater certainty, a reference to sexual activity in subsection (5) includes a reference to an act of bestiality, as defined in subsection 160(7).

Section.Text: Paragraph 172.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is, or who the accused believes is, under the age of 18 years, for the purpose of facilitating the commission of an offence with respect to that person under subsection 153(1), section 155, 163.1, 170, 171 or 279.011 or subsection 279.02(2), 279.03(2), 286.1(2), 286.2(2) or 286.3(2) or the commission of an offence with respect to that person under section 346 of a sexual nature or for a sexual purpose;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 173(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person who, in any place, for a sexual purpose, exposes their sexual organs to a person who is under the age of 16 years
Paragraph 173(2)(a) of the Act is amended by replacing âa term of not more than two yearsâ with âa term of not more than 10 yearsâ.
Paragraph 173(2)(b) of the Act is amended by replacing âa term of not more than six monthsâ with âa term of not more than two years less a dayâ.
Paragraph (a) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (xxvii):

Subparagraph.Label: (xxvii.01)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 160(3.1) (representation of bestiality),

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(xxvii.2) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xxvii.2)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 162.1(1) (intimate image â publication, etc.),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxvii.3)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 162.1(1.1) (intimate image â threat to publish, etc.),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (xxix):

Subparagraph.Label: (xxix.01)
Subparagraph.Text: section 169.1 (recruitment â young person),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (xxxix.2):

Subparagraph.Label: (xxxix.3)
Subparagraph.Text: section 264 (criminal harassment),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (xxxix.3):

Subparagraph.Label: (xxxix.4)
Subparagraph.Text: section 264.01 (coercion or control of intimate partner),

Section.Text: Subsection 231(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Femicide, including of intimate partner, and other aggravated circumstances
Subsection.Text: Irrespective of whether a murder is planned and deliberate on the part of any person, murder is first degree murder when the death is caused by that person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: while engaging in, or after having engaged in, a pattern of coercive or controlling conduct with intent to cause the victim to believe that the victimâs physical or psychological safety is threatened, in the case where the victim is that personâs intimate partner;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: while exercising control, direction or influence over the movements of the victim with intent to exploit the victim, within the meaning of section 279.04;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: while committing or attempting to commit an offence of a sexual nature or an offence for a sexual purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: while motivated by hate based on colour, race, religion, national or ethnic origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or mental or physical disability.

Subsection.Text: Irrespective of whether a murder is planned and deliberate on the part of any person, murder is first degree murder when the death is caused by that person while committing or attempting to commit an offence under section 264 (criminal harassment) with intent to cause the victim to believe that the victimâs physical or psychological safety, or the physical or psychological safety of anyone known to the victim, is threatened.

Section.Text: Section 236 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 236(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Femicide, including of intimate partner, and other aggravated circumstances
Subsection.Text: The court that imposes a sentence for manslaughter shall consider imposing a sentence of imprisonment for life if the person committed the manslaughter

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: while engaging in, or after having engaged in, a pattern of coercive or controlling conduct with intent to cause the victim to believe that the victimâs physical or psychological safety is threatened, in the case where the victim is that personâs intimate partner;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: while exercising control, direction or influence over the movements of the victim with intent to exploit the victim, within the meaning of section 279.04;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: while committing or attempting to commit an offence of a sexual nature or an offence for a sexual purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: while motivated by hate based on colour, race, religion, national or ethnic origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or mental or physical disability.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 264(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Everyone commits an offence who, with intent to harass another person or being reckless as to whether they could be harassing another person, engages without lawful authority in conduct referred to in subsection (2) if, in all the circumstances, the conduct could reasonably be expected to cause that other person to believe that the other personâs safety, or the safety of anyone known to the other person, is threatened.
The portion of subsection 264(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The conduct mentioned in subsection (1) consists of doing any of the following in person, by a means of telecommunication or by any other means:
Subsection 264(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: monitoring the location, movements, actions or social interactions of the other person or of anyone known to them;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 264(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in threatening conduct directed at the other person, at anyone known to them or at any animal that is in their care or is their property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 264(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person who commits an offence under this section is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 264(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the terms or conditions of an order made under section 161 or a recognizance entered into under section 810, 810.03, 810.1 or 810.2; or

Subsection.Text: Section 264 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
For the purposes of this section, and for greater certainty, a personâs safety includes their psychological safety.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 264:

Section.MarginalNote: Coercion or control of intimate partner

Subsection.Text: Everyone commits an offence who engages in a pattern of coercive or controlling conduct referred to in subsection (2), with intent to cause their intimate partner to believe that the intimate partnerâs safety is threatened or knowing that, or being reckless as to whether, the pattern of coercive or controlling conduct would cause their intimate partner to believe that the intimate partnerâs safety is threatened.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Pattern of coercive or controlling conduct
Subsection.Text: A pattern of coercive or controlling conduct consists of any combination, or repeated instances, of any of the following acts:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: using, attempting to use or threatening to use violence against

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the intimate partner,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any person under the age of 18 who is the intimate partnerâs child or who is in the intimate partnerâs lawful care or charge,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any other person known to the intimate partner, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any animal that is in the care or is the property of the intimate partner;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: coercing or attempting to coerce the intimate partner to engage in sexual activity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: engaging in any other conduct â including conduct listed in any of the following subparagraphs â if, in all the circumstances, the conduct could reasonably be expected to cause the intimate partner to believe that the intimate partnerâs safety, or the safety of anyone known to them, is threatened:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: controlling, attempting to control or monitoring the intimate partnerâs location, movements, actions or social interactions, including by a means of telecommunication,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: controlling or attempting to control the manner in which the intimate partner cares for any person under the age of 18 referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii) or any animal referred to in subparagraph (a)(iv),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: controlling or attempting to control any matter related to the intimate partnerâs employment or education,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: controlling or attempting to control the intimate partnerâs finances or other property or monitoring their finances,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: controlling or attempting to control the intimate partnerâs expression of gender, physical appearance, manner of dress, diet, taking of medication or access to health services or to medication,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: controlling or attempting to control the intimate partnerâs expression of their thoughts, their opinions, their religious, spiritual or other beliefs, or their culture, including the intimate partnerâs use of their language or their access to their linguistic, religious, spiritual or cultural community, or

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: threatening to die by suicide or to self-harm.

Subsection.Text: The circumstances referred to in paragraph (2)(c) include the nature of the relationship between the accused and the intimate partner, in particular whether the intimate partner is in a position of vulnerability in relation to the accused and whether the accused manipulated the intimate partner by targeting their vulnerabilities.
Everyone who commits an offence under this section is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, and for greater certainty, a personâs safety includes their psychological safety.

Section.Text: Paragraph 271(b) of the Act is amended by replacing â18 monthsâ by âtwo years less a dayâ.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 275:

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 276(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

In proceedings in respect of an offence under section 151, 152, 153, 153.1 or 155, subsection 160(2) or (3) or section 170, 171, 172, 173, 271, 272 or 273 or any other offence under this Act that is of a sexual nature or that is committed for a sexual purpose, evidence that the complainant has engaged in sexual activity, whether with the accused or with any other person, is not admissible to support an inference that, by reason of the sexual nature of that activity, the complainant
Subsection 276(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In proceedings in respect of an offence referred to in subsection (1), evidence that the complainant has engaged in sexual activity, other than the sexual activity that forms the subject matter of the charge, whether with the accused or with any other person, shall not be adduced unless the judge, provincial court judge or justice determines, in accordance with the procedures set out in sections 276.01, 276.06, 276.1, 278.3 and 278.35, as the case may be, that the evidence is not being adduced for the purpose of supporting an inference described in subsection (1), is relevant to an issue at trial, is of specific instances of sexual activity and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if sought to be adduced by or on behalf of the accused, has significant probative value that is not substantially outweighed by the danger of prejudice to the proper administration of justice; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if sought to be adduced by or on behalf of the prosecutor, has probative value that is not outweighed by the danger of prejudice to the proper administration of justice.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 276(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Il est entendu que, pour lâapplication du prÃ©sent article, activitÃ© sexuelle sâentend notamment de toute communication effectuÃ©e dans un but sexuel ou dont le contenu est de nature sexuelle.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 276:

Subsection.Text: Application may be made to the judge, provincial court judge or justice by or on behalf of the accused for a hearing under section 276.02 to determine whether evidence is admissible under subsection 276(2).

The application must be made in writing and be accompanied by an affidavit setting out detailed particulars of the evidence that the accused seeks to adduce and the relevance of that evidence to an issue at trial, and a copy of the application and the affidavit must be given to the prosecutor and the complainant and filed with the clerk of the court.
The judge, provincial court judge or justice shall consider the application with the jury and the public excluded.

If the judge, provincial court judge or justice is satisfied that the application was made in accordance with subsection (2), that a copy of the application was given to the prosecutor and to the complainant and filed with the clerk of the court at least 60 days previously or within any shorter interval that the judge, provincial court judge or justice may allow in the interests of justice, and that the evidence sought to be adduced is capable of being admissible under subsection 276(2), the judge, provincial court judge or justice shall grant the application and hold a hearing under section 276.02 to determine whether the evidence is admissible under subsection 276(2).
The condition in subsection (4) that a copy of the application is given to the complainant is not met if the accused personally gives a copy of it to the complainant.

Section.MarginalNote: Hearing â jury and public excluded

Subsection.Text: The jury and the public shall be excluded from a hearing to determine whether evidence is admissible under subsection 276(2).
The complainant is not a compellable witness at the hearing but may appear and make submissions.
The judge, provincial court judge or justice shall, as soon as feasible, inform the complainant who participates in the hearing of their right to be represented by counsel.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the judge, provincial court judge or justice shall determine whether the evidence, or any part of it, is admissible under subsection 276(2) and shall provide reasons for that determination, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if not all of the evidence is to be admitted, the reasons must state the part of the evidence that is to be admitted;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons must state the factors referred to in subsection 276(3) that affected the determination; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if all or any part of the evidence is to be admitted, the reasons must state the manner in which that evidence is expected to be relevant to an issue at trial.

Subsection.Text: The reasons provided under subsection (4) must be entered in the record of the proceedings or, if the proceedings are not recorded, must be provided in writing.
A person shall not publish in any document, or broadcast or transmit in any way, any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contents of an application made under subsection 276.01(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any evidence taken, the information given or the representations made at the consideration of an application made under subsection 276.01(1) or at a hearing under section 276.02;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the decision of a judge, provincial court judge or justice under subsection 276.01(4), unless the judge, provincial court judge or justice, after taking into account the complainantâs right of privacy and the interests of justice, orders that the decision may be published, broadcast or transmitted;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the determination made and the reasons provided under subsection 276.02(4), unless that determination is that evidence is admissible or the judge, provincial court judge or justice, after taking into account the complainantâs right of privacy and the interests of justice, orders that the determination and the reasons may be published, broadcast or transmitted.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1) is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Section.MarginalNote: Instruction to jury â use of evidence
Section.Text: If evidence is admitted at trial on the basis of a determination made under subsection 276.02(4), the judge shall instruct the jury as to the uses that the jury may and may not make of that evidence.
For the purposes of sections 675 and 676, a determination made under subsection 276.02(4) is deemed to be a question of law.

Subsection.Text: Application may be made to the judge, provincial court judge or justice by the prosecutor for a hearing to determine whether evidence is admissible under subsection 276(2).
The application must be made in writing and must set out detailed particulars of the evidence that the prosecutor seeks to adduce and the relevance of that evidence to an issue at trial.

The application need not be supported by any affidavit or oral testimony of the complainant or of any other person with knowledge of the complainantâs sexual history, any transcript from any preliminary inquiry or any sworn police statement.

A copy of the application must be given to the accused and filed with the clerk of the court at least 60 days before the hearing or within any shorter interval that the judge, provincial court judge or justice may allow in the interests of justice.
The jury and the public shall be excluded from the hearing.
The complainant is not a compellable witness at the hearing.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the judge, provincial court judge or justice shall determine whether the evidence, or any part of it, is admissible under subsection 276(2) and shall provide reasons for that determination, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if not all of the evidence is to be admitted, the reasons must state the part of the evidence that is to be admitted;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons must state the factors referred to in subsection 276(3) that affected the determination; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if all or any part of the evidence is to be admitted, the reasons must state the manner in which that evidence is expected to be relevant to an issue at trial.

Subsection.Text: The reasons provided under subsection (7) must be entered in the record of the proceedings or, if the proceedings are not recorded, must be provided in writing.
A person shall not publish in any document, or broadcast or transmit in any way, any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contents of an application made under subsection 276.06(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any evidence taken, the information given or the representations made at the consideration of an application made under subsection 276.06(1) or at a hearing under section 276.06;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the determination made and the reasons provided under subsection 276.06(7), unless that determination is that evidence is admissible or the judge, provincial court judge or justice, after taking into account the complainantâs right of privacy and the interests of justice, orders that the determination and the reasons may be published, broadcast or transmitted.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1) is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Section.MarginalNote: Instruction to jury â use of evidence
Section.Text: If evidence is admitted at trial on the basis of a determination made under subsection 276.06(7), the judge shall instruct the jury as to the uses that the jury may and may not make of that evidence.
For the purposes of sections 675 and 676, a determination made under subsection 276.06(7) is deemed to be a question of law.

Subsection.Text: The prosecutor, the accused and the complainant may jointly apply to the judge, provincial court judge or justice for a determination as to whether any evidence referred to in subsection 276(1) is admissible under subsection 276(2) without holding a hearing under section 276.02.
The application must be made in writing and signed by the applicants and must set out the detailed particulars of the evidence and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the relevance of that evidence to an issue at trial;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: how the conditions for admissibility referred to in subsection 276(2) are satisfied; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any information the applicants consider necessary to assist the judge, provincial court judge or justice when the judge, provincial court judge or justice takes into account the factors referred to in subsection 276(3).

Subsection.Text: A copy of the application must be filed with the clerk of the court at least 60 days before the trial.

The judge, provincial court judge or justice shall, in the absence of the applicants, and without holding a hearing, consider the application and make a determination no later than 30 days after the day on which the application is made as to whether the evidence, or any part of it, is admissible under subsection 276(2) and shall provide reasons for that determination, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if not all of the evidence is to be admitted, the reasons must state the part of the evidence that is to be admitted;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons must state the factors referred to in subsection 276(3) that affected the determination; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if all or any part of the evidence is to be admitted, the reasons must state the manner in which that evidence is expected to be relevant to an issue at trial.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Power to grant application or hold hearing
Subsection.Text: If the judge, provincial court judge or justice is satisfied that the evidence of sexual activity is admissible under subsection 276(2), taking into account the factors set out in subsection 276(3), they shall grant the application. If they are not so satisfied, they shall hold a hearing under section 276.02 to determine whether the evidence is admissible under subsection 276(2).

If evidence of sexual activity is determined to be admissible, the judge, provincial court judge or justice shall direct the applicants as to the uses that the they may and may not make of that evidence.
A person shall not publish in any document, or broadcast or transmit in any way, any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contents of an application made under subsection 276.1(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any evidence taken, the information given or the representations made at the consideration of an application made under subsection 276.1(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the determination made and the reasons provided under section 276.1, unless that determination is that evidence is admissible or the judge, provincial court judge or justice, after taking into account the complainantâs right of privacy and the interests of justice, orders that the determination and the reasons may be published, broadcast or transmitted.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1) is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Section.MarginalNote: Instruction to jury â use of evidence
Section.Text: If evidence is admitted at trial on the basis of a determination made under subsection 276.1(4), the judge shall instruct the jury as to the uses that the jury may and may not make of that evidence.
For the purposes of sections 675 and 676, a determination made under subsection 276.1(4) or (5) is deemed to be a question of law.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 277:
Sections 278.1 to 278.97 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Production and Admissibility of Records and Therapeutic Records

Text.Ins: The following definitions apply in sections 278.11 to 278.36.

Definition.Text: recordâmeans any form of record that contains personal information for which there is a reasonable expectation of privacy and includes medical, education, employment, child welfare, adoption and social services records, personal journals and diaries, and any record containing personal information the production or disclosure of which is protected by any other Act of Parliament or a provincial legislature, but does not include records made by persons responsible for the investigation or prosecution of the offence.â(dossier)

therapeutic recordâmeans any form of record, regardless of its contents, that was produced in the course of psychiatric treatment or any therapy or counselling provided by a health care professional who is entitled under the laws of a province or of a foreign state to provide the treatment, therapy or counselling.â(dossier thÃ©rapeutique)

Heading.TitleText: Production to Accused of Records and Therapeutic Records in the Possession of Third Party

Section.MarginalNote: Records and therapeutic records possessed by third party

Subsection.Text: Except in accordance with sections 278.12 to 278.19, no record or therapeutic record relating to a complainant or a witness that is in the possession or control of a third party shall be produced to an accused in any proceedings in respect of any of the following offences or in any proceedings in respect of two or more offences at least one of which is any of the following offences:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 151, 152, 153, 153.1, 155, 160, 170, 171, 172, 173, 213, 271, 272, 273, 279.01, 279.011, 279.02, 279.03, 286.1, 286.2 or 286.3 or any other offence under this Act that is of a sexual nature or that is committed for a sexual purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any offence under this Act, as it read from time to time before the day on which this paragraph comes into force, if the conduct alleged would be an offence referred to in paragraph (a) if it occurred on or after that day.

Subsection.Text: In subsection (1), third party means a person other than the prosecutor or the accused.

An accused who seeks production of a record or therapeutic record referred to in section 278.11 must make an application for its production to the judge before whom the accused is to be, or is being, tried.

For greater certainty, the application referred to in subsection (1) must not be made to a judge, provincial court judge or justice presiding at any other proceedings, including a preliminary inquiry.
The application must be made in writing and must set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: particulars identifying the record or the therapeutic record that the accused seeks to have produced and the name of the person who has possession or control of it;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a record, the grounds on which the accused relies to establish that the record is likely relevant to an issue at trial or to the competence of a witness to testify; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a therapeutic record, the grounds on which the accused relies to establish that the therapeutic record contains evidence that could raise a reasonable doubt as to the accusedâs guilt.

Subsection.Text: Any one or more of the following assertions by the accused are not sufficient on their own to establish that the record is likely relevant to an issue at trial or to the competence of a witness to testify or that the therapeutic record contains evidence that could raise a reasonable doubt as to the accusedâs guilt:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record exists;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to medical or psychiatric treatment, therapy or counselling that the complainant or witness has received or is receiving;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the incident that is the subject matter of the proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may disclose a prior inconsistent statement of the complainant or witness;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may relate to the credibility of the complainant or witness;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may relate to the reliability of the testimony of the complainant or witness merely because the complainant or witness has received or is receiving psychiatric treatment, therapy or counselling;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may reveal allegations of sexual abuse of the complainant by a person other than the accused;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the sexual activity of the complainant with any person, including the accused;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the presence or absence of a recent complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the complainantâs sexual reputation;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record was made close in time to a complaint or to the activity that forms the subject matter of the charge against the accused.

Subsection.Text: The accused shall serve the application on the prosecutor, on the person who has possession or control of the record or therapeutic record, on the complainant or witness, as the case may be, and on any other person to whom, to the knowledge of the accused, the record or therapeutic record relates, at least 60 days before the hearing referred to in subsection 278.13(1) or within any shorter interval that the judge may allow in the interests of justice. The accused shall also serve a subpoena issued under Part XXII in Form 16.1 on the person who has possession or control of the record or therapeutic record at the same time as the application is served.
The judge may at any time order that the application be served on any person to whom the judge considers the record or therapeutic record may relate.
The service requirement on the accused in subsection (5) is not fulfilled if the accused personally serves the application or a subpoena on the complainant.
The judge shall hold a hearing in camera to determine whether to order the person who has possession or control of the record or therapeutic record to produce it to the court for review by the judge.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Persons who may appear at hearing
Subsection.Text: The person who has possession or control of the record or therapeutic record, the complainant or witness, as the case may be, and any other person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates may appear and make submissions at the hearing, but they are not compellable as witnesses at the hearing.
The judge shall, as soon as feasible, inform any person referred to in subsection (2) who participates in the hearing of their right to be represented by counsel.
An order for costs must not be made against a person referred to in subsection (2) in respect of their participation in the hearing.

The judge may order the person who has possession or control of the record or therapeutic record to produce it, or any part of it, to the court for review by the judge if, after the hearing referred to in section 278.13, the judge is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the application was made in accordance with subsections 278.12(2) to (6);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a record, the accused has established that it is likely relevant to an issue at trial or to the competence of a witness to testify or, in case of a therapeutic record, the accused has established that it contains evidence that could raise a reasonable doubt as to the accusedâs guilt; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the production of all or any part of the record or therapeutic record is necessary in the interests of justice.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether to order the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, for review, the judge shall consider the salutary and deleterious effects of the determination on the accusedâs right to make a full answer and defence and on the right to privacy, personal security and equality of the complainant or witness, as the case may be, and of any other person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates. In particular, the judge shall take the following factors into account:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the extent to which the record or therapeutic record is necessary for the accused to make a full answer and defence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the probative value of the record or therapeutic record;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and extent of the reasonable expectation of privacy with respect to the record or therapeutic record;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: whether production of the record or therapeutic record is based on a discriminatory belief or bias;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the potential prejudice to the personal dignity and right to privacy of any person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: societyâs interest in encouraging the reporting of sexual offences;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: societyâs interest in encouraging the obtaining of treatment by complainants of sexual offences; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the effect of the determination on the integrity of the trial process.

Subsection.Text: If the judge has ordered the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, for review, the judge shall review it, or the part of it, in the absence of the parties in order to determine whether the record or therapeutic record, or the part, should be produced to the accused.
The judge may hold a hearing in camera if the judge considers that it will assist in making the determination.
Subsections 278.13(2) to (4) apply in the case of a hearing under subsection (2).

Section.MarginalNote: Order â Production of record to accused

Subsection.Text: If the judge is satisfied that the record, or part of it, is likely relevant to an issue at trial or to the competence of a witness to testify and that its production is necessary in the interests of justice, the judge may order that the record, or the part, that is likely relevant be produced to the accused, subject to any conditions that may be imposed under subsection (4).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Order â Production of therapeutic record to accused
Subsection.Text: If the judge is satisfied that the therapeutic record, or part of it, contains evidence that is likely to raise a reasonable doubt as to the accusedâs guilt and that is not available from any other source, the judge may order that the therapeutic record, or the part, be produced to the accused, subject to any conditions that may be imposed under subsection (4).

In determining whether to order the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, to the accused, the judge shall consider the salutary and deleterious effects of the determination on the accusedâs right to make a full answer and defence and on the right to privacy, personal security and equality of the complainant or witness, as the case may be, and of any other person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates and, in particular, shall take the factors set out in paragraphs 278.14(2)(a) to (h) into account.

If the judge orders the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, to the accused, the judge may impose conditions on the production to protect the interests of justice and, to the greatest extent possible, the privacy, personal security and equality interests of the complainant or witness, as the case may be, and of any other person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates, including, for example, the following conditions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record be edited as directed by the judge;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that a copy of the record or therapeutic record, rather than the original, be produced;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the accused and counsel for the accused not disclose the contents of the record or therapeutic record to any other person, except with the approval of the court;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record be viewed only at the offices of the court;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that no copies of the record or therapeutic record be made or that restrictions be imposed on the number of copies of it that may be made; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that information regarding any person named in the record or therapeutic record, such as their address, telephone number and place of employment, be severed from the record or therapeutic record.

Subsection.Text: If the judge orders the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, to the accused, the judge shall direct that a copy of the record or therapeutic record, or the part, be provided to the prosecutor, unless the judge determines that it is not in the interests of justice to do so.
The record or therapeutic record, or the part of it, that is produced to the accused under an order under subsection (1) must not be used in any other proceedings.

If the judge refuses to order the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, to the accused, the record or therapeutic record, or the part, must, unless a court orders otherwise, be kept in a sealed package by the court until the later of the end of the time for any appeal and the completion of any appeal in the proceedings against the accused, at which time the record or therapeutic record, or the part, must be returned to the person lawfully entitled to possession or control of it.
The judge shall provide reasons for ordering or refusing to order the production of the record or therapeutic record, or part of it, under subsection 278.14(1) or 278.16(1) or (2).
The reasons provided under subsection (1) must be entered in the record of the proceedings or, if the proceedings are not recorded, must be provided in writing.
No person shall publish in any document, or broadcast or transmit in any way, any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contents of an application made under subsection 278.12(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any evidence taken, information given or submissions made at a hearing under subsection 278.13(1) or 278.15(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the determination of the judge under subsection 278.14(1) or 278.16(1) or (2) and the reasons provided under section 278.17, unless the judge, after taking into account the interests of justice and the right to privacy of the person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates, orders that the determination and the reasons may be published, broadcast or transmitted.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes subsection (1) is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Section.Text: For the purposes of sections 675 and 676, a determination to make or refuse to make an order under subsection 278.14(1) or 278.16(1) or (2) is deemed to be a question of law.

Heading.TitleText: Production to Accused of Records and Therapeutic Records in the Possession of the Prosecutor

Section.MarginalNote: Records and therapeutic records possessed by prosecutor

Subsection.Text: Except in accordance with sections 278.21 to 278.28, no record or therapeutic record relating to a complainant or a witness that is in the possession or control of the prosecutor shall be produced to an accused in any proceedings in respect of any of the following offences or in any proceedings in respect of two or more offences at least one of which is any of the following offences:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence under section 151, 152, 153, 153.1, 155, 160, 170, 171, 172, 173, 213, 271, 272, 273, 279.01, 279.011, 279.02, 279.03, 286.1, 286.2 or 286.3 or any other offence under this Act that is of a sexual nature or that is committed for a sexual purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any offence under this Act, as it read from time to time before the day on which this paragraph comes into force, if the conduct alleged would be an offence referred to in paragraph (a) if it occurred on or after that day.

Subsection.Text: The prosecutor may disclose to the accused

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a record or part of a record, if the prosecutor intends to adduce the record or part in court or if the record or part directly relates to the activity that forms the subject matter of the charge against the accused;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a record or therapeutic record, or a part of one, if the complainant or witness to whom it relates agrees to its disclosure to the accused; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any communication between the accused and the complainant.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty of prosecutor to give notice
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), if a record or therapeutic record in respect of which this section applies is in the possession or control of the prosecutor, the prosecutor shall notify the accused that it is in the prosecutorâs possession or control but, in doing so, the prosecutor shall not disclose its contents.
Disclosure under subsection (2) does not affect the application of sections 278.29 to 278.38.

An accused who seeks production of a record or therapeutic record referred to in subsection 278.2(1) must make an application for its production to the judge before whom the accused is to be, or is being, tried.

For greater certainty, the application referred to in subsection (1) must not be made to a judge, provincial court judge or justice presiding at any other proceedings, including a preliminary inquiry.
The application must be made in writing and must set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: particulars identifying the record or the therapeutic record that the accused seeks to have produced;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a record, the grounds on which the accused relies to establish that the record is likely relevant to an issue at trial or to the competence of a witness to testify; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a therapeutic record, the grounds on which the accused relies to establish that the therapeutic record contains evidence that could raise a reasonable doubt as to the accusedâs guilt.

Subsection.Text: Any one or more of the following assertions by the accused are not sufficient on their own to establish that the record is likely relevant to an issue at trial or to the competence of a witness to testify or that the therapeutic record contains evidence that could raise a reasonable doubt as to the accusedâs guilt:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record exists;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to medical or psychiatric treatment, therapy or counselling that the complainant or witness has received or is receiving;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the incident that is the subject matter of the proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may disclose a prior inconsistent statement of the complainant or witness;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may relate to the credibility of the complainant or witness;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may relate to the reliability of the testimony of the complainant or witness merely because the complainant or witness has received or is receiving psychiatric treatment, therapy or counselling;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record may reveal allegations of sexual abuse of the complainant by a person other than the accused;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the sexual activity of the complainant with any person, including the accused;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the presence or absence of a recent complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record relates to the complainantâs sexual reputation;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: that the record or therapeutic record was made close in time to a complaint or to the activity that forms the subject matter of the charge against the accused.

Subsection.Text: The accused shall serve the application on the prosecutor, on the complainant or witness, as the case may be, and on any other person to whom, to the knowledge of the accused, the record or therapeutic record relates, at least 60 days before the hearing referred to in subsection 278.22(1) or within any shorter interval that the judge may allow in the interests of justice.
The judge may at any time order that the application be served on any person to whom the judge considers the record or therapeutic record may relate.
The service requirement on the accused in subsection (5) is not fulfilled if the accused personally serves the application the complainant.
The judge shall hold a hearing in camera to determine whether to order the prosecutor to produce the record or therapeutic record to the court for review by the judge.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Persons who may appear at hearing
Subsection.Text: The complainant or witness, as the case may be, and any other person to whom the record or therapeutic record relates may appear and make submissions at the hearing, but they are not compellable as witnesses at the hearing.
The judge shall, as soon as feasible, inform any person referred to in subsection (2) who participates in the hearing of their right to be represented by counsel.
An order for costs must not be made against a person referred to in subsection (2) in respect of their participation in the hearing.
